---
title: "project_q3_q4_q5"
author: "Mohammad Faris"
date: "2025-11-20"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r}
df <- Nutrition_BMINDS_Food_Insecurity_Survey_and_stress_Responses_before_u_pdate
```

```{r}
head(df)
```

```{r}
colnames(df)
```

```{r}
student_col <- "Are you currently enrolled in college?"
gpa_col <- "What is your cumulative GPA range?"
income_col <- "What is the total income of your primary household?"

k10_cols <- c(
  "In the last month, how often have you been upset because of something that happened unexpectedly?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt that you were unable to control the important things in your life?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt nervous and stressed?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt confident about your ability to handle your personal problems?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt that things were going your way?",
  "In the last month, how often have you found that you could not cope with all the things that you had to do?",
  "In the last month, how often have you been able to control irritations in your life?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt that you were on top of things?",
  "In the last month, how often have you been angered because of things that happened that were outside of your control?",
  "In the last month, how often have you felt difficulties were piling up so high that you could not overcome them?"
)

rs_cols <- c(
  "1. I tend to bounce back quickly after hard times",
  "2. I have a hard time making it through stressful events",
  "3. It does not take me long to recover from a stressful event.",
  "4. It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens.",
  "5. I usually come through difficult times with little trouble.",
  "6. I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life."
)

fies_cols <- c(
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you were worried you would not have enough food to eat?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you were unable to eat healthy and nutritious food?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you ate only a few kinds of foods?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you had to skip a meal?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you ate less than you thought you should?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources your household ran out of food?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you were hungry but did not eat?",
  "During the last 12 months, was there a time when, because of lack of money or other resources you went without eating for a whole day?"
)

food_cols <- c(
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume whole grain products? (e.g., whole grain bread, whole grain cereal, brown rice or whole wheat pasta",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume dairy products?(e.g., milk, cheese, yogurt …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume coffee or other sources of caffeine?(e.g., soda, tea, chocolate, energy drink …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fruits?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume nuts, including flaxseed?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume rice and/or pasta?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume red meat, chicken, or turkey?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume dark green leafy vegetables?(e.g., spinach, kale, lettuce …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume beans?(e.g. black beans, black-eyed peas, lentils, lima beans, soy nuts …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fish and/or shellfish (including sardines and tuna)?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fast foods and/or pre-made or packaged food?",
  "On average week, how many times do you take multivitamin supplements?",
  "On average week, how many times do you take fish oil supplements?"
)

 
all_columns_to_keep <- c(student_col, gpa_col, income_col, k10_cols, rs_cols, fies_cols, food_cols)

data <- df %>%
  select(all_of(all_columns_to_keep))

print(paste("Num of col in new data:", ncol(data)))
glimpse(data)
```

```{r}
# to calculate the mode
get_mode <- function(v) {
  uniqv <- unique(na.omit(v))
  uniqv[which.max(tabulate(match(v, uniqv)))]
}

# select
cols_to_fill <- c(student_col, gpa_col, income_col, k10_cols, rs_cols, fies_cols, food_cols)


data <- data %>%
  # Delete the NA record in Are you currently enrolled in college? (because it is just one record)
  filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)) %>%
  # fill it
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_fill), ~ifelse(is.na(.), get_mode(.), .)))

# Check
sum(is.na(data[cols_to_fill]))
```

# Q3. Do university students feel better than people who are not studying?

```{r}
students <- data %>% filter(`Are you currently enrolled in college?` == "Yes")
non_students <- data %>% filter(`Are you currently enrolled in college?` == "No")
```

```{r}
head(students)
head(non_students)
```

```{r}
unique_k10_values <- lapply(data[k10_cols], unique)
unique_k10_values
```

```{r}
data[k10_cols] <- lapply(data[k10_cols], function(x) {
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- trimws(x)
  return(x)
})
```

```{r}
lapply(data[k10_cols], unique)
```

```{r}
mapping <- c(
  "never" = 1,
  "almost never" = 2,
  "sometimes" = 3,
  "fairly often" = 4,
  "very often" = 5
)

data[k10_cols] <- lapply(data[k10_cols], function(x) {
  as.numeric(mapping[x])
})
```

```{r}
summary(data[k10_cols])
```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(data[k10_cols]))
```

```{r}
data$K10_total <- rowSums(data[k10_cols])
```

```{r}
summary(data$K10_total)
```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(data[rs_cols]))
```

```{r}
negative_rs_cols <- c(
  "2. I have a hard time making it through stressful events",
  "4. It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens.",
  "6. I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life."
)

# reverse coding
data[negative_rs_cols] <- 6 - data[negative_rs_cols]
```

```{r}
data$RS_total <- rowSums(data[rs_cols])
```

```{r}
summary(data$RS_total)
```

```{r}
summary_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_K10_Distress = mean(K10_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Resilience = mean(RS_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count = n()
  )

print(summary_stats)
```

```{r}
summary_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_K10_Distress = mean(K10_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Resilience = mean(RS_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count = n()
  )

print(summary_stats)
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)),
  aes(x = K10_total)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of K10 Scores", 
    subtitle = "Checking distribution shape for Students vs Non-Students"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)),
  aes(x = RS_total)
) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of RS Scores", 
    subtitle = "Checking distribution shape for Students vs Non-Students"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)),
  aes(sample = K10_total)
) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plot for K10 Scores") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)),
  aes(sample = RS_total)
) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~`Are you currently enrolled in college?`) +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plot for RS Scores") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
shapiro.test(data$K10_total[data$`Are you currently enrolled in college?` == "Yes"])
```

```{r}
shapiro.test(data$RS_total[data$`Are you currently enrolled in college?` == "Yes"])
```

```{r}
wilcox.test(K10_total ~ `Are you currently enrolled in college?`, data = data)
```

```{r}
wilcox.test(RS_total ~ `Are you currently enrolled in college?`, data = data)
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(`Are you currently enrolled in college?`)),
  aes(
    x = `Are you currently enrolled in college?`,
    y = K10_total,
    fill = `Are you currently enrolled in college?`
  )
) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Mental Distress (K10) Scores",
    subtitle = "Students vs. Non-Students (Lower score means feeling better)",
    x = "Enrolled in College?",
    y = "K10 Distress Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Yes" = "#69b3a2", "No" = "#404080"))

```

# Q4. What is the connection between having enough food and students’ GPA?

```{r}
summary(data$`What is your cumulative GPA range?`)
```

```{r}
head(data$`What is your cumulative GPA range?`)
```

```{r}
gpa_col <- "What is your cumulative GPA range?"
```

```{r}
unique_gpa_values <- lapply(data[gpa_col], unique)
unique_gpa_values
```

```{r}
data$GPA_factor <- factor(data$`What is your cumulative GPA range?`,
                          levels = c("Less than 1","1.0-1.49","1.5-1.99",
                                     "2.0-2.29","2.3-2.69","2.7-2.99",
                                     "3.0-3.39","3.4-3.69","3.7-4.0"),
                          ordered = TRUE)

```

```{r}
table(data$GPA_factor, useNA = "ifany")
```

```{r}
unique_fies_values <- lapply(data[fies_cols], unique)
unique_fies_values
```

```{r}
# 
data[fies_cols] <- lapply(data[fies_cols], function(x) {
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- trimws(x)
  return(x)
})
```

```{r}
lapply(data[fies_cols], unique)
```

```{r}
mapping <- c(
  "no" = 0,
  "not sure or don't know" = 0.5,
  "yes" = 1
)

data[fies_cols] <- lapply(data[fies_cols], function(x) {
  as.numeric(mapping[x])
})
```

```{r}
summary(data[fies_cols])
```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(data[fies_cols]))
```

```{r}
data$FIES_total <- rowSums(data[fies_cols])
```

```{r}
summary(data$FIES_total)
```

```{r}
students_data <- data %>%
  filter(`Are you currently enrolled in college?` == "Yes" & !is.na(GPA_factor))
```

```{r}
students_data$GPA_factor <- factor(students_data$GPA_factor, 
                                   levels = c("Less than 1",
                                              "1.0-1.49",
                                              "1.5-1.99",
                                              "2.0-2.29",
                                              "2.3-2.69",
                                              "2.7-2.99",
                                              "3.0-3.39",
                                              "3.4-3.69",
                                              "3.7-4.0"),
                                   ordered = TRUE)
```

```{r}
shapiro.test(students_data$FIES_total)
```

```{r}
lm_model <- lm(FIES_total ~ GPA_factor, data = students_data)

summary(lm_model)
```

```{r}
students_data %>%
  group_by(GPA_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Food_Insecurity = mean(FIES_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    Student_Count = n()
  )
```

```{r}
students_summary <- students_data %>%
  group_by(GPA_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_FIES = mean(FIES_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE = sd(FIES_total, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()) 
  )


ggplot(students_summary, aes(x = GPA_factor, y = Mean_FIES)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#d95f02", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.7) +  
  labs(title = "Higher Grades Associated with Lower Food Insecurity",
       subtitle = "Average FIES Score by GPA (Shorter bar is better)",
       x = "Cumulative GPA",
       y = "Average Food Insecurity Score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +   
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) 

```

```{r}
new_labels <- c(
  "Less than 1" = "<1",
  "1.0-1.49"    = "1.2",
  "1.5-1.99"    = "1.8",
  "2.0-2.29"    = "2.1",
  "2.3-2.69"    = "2.4",
  "2.7-2.99"    = "2.8",
  "3.0-3.39"    = "3.2",
  "3.4-3.69"    = "3.5",
  "3.7-4.0"     = "3.9"
)

ggplot(students_data, aes(x = GPA_factor, y = FIES_total)) +
  # Boxplot
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  
  scale_x_discrete(labels = new_labels) +
  
  labs(title = "Relationship: GPA vs. Food Insecurity",
       subtitle = "Higher GPA is associated with lower food insecurity",
       x = "Cumulative GPA",
       y = "Food Insecurity Score (FIES)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  coord_flip() 
```

# Q5.How does family income affect the kind of food people eat?

```{r}
unique_income_values <- lapply(data[income_col], unique)
unique_income_values
```

```{r}
data$income_factor <- factor(data$`What is the total income of your primary household?`,
                          levels = c("Less than $10,000",
                                     "$10,000 to $19,999", 
                                     "$20,000 to $29,999",
                                     "$30,000 to $39,999",
                                     "$40,000 to $49,999",
                                     "$50,000 to $59,999",
                                     "$60,000 to $69,999",
                                     "$70,000 to $79,999",
                                     "$80,000 to $89,999",
                                     "$90,000 to $99,999",
                                     "$100,000 to $149,999",
                                     "$150,000 to $249,999",
                                     "$250,000 or more"),
                          ordered = TRUE)
```

```{r}
unique_food_values <- lapply(data[food_cols], unique)
unique_food_values
```

```{r}
data[food_cols] <- lapply(data[food_cols], function(x) {
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- trimws(x)
  return(x)
})
```

```{r}
lapply(data[food_cols], unique)
```

```{r}
mapping <- c(
  "never" = 0,
  "1 time" = 1,
  "2 times" = 2,
  "3 times" = 3,
  "4 times" = 4,
  "5 times or more" = 5
)

data[food_cols] <- lapply(data[food_cols], function(x) {
  as.numeric(mapping[x])
})
```

```{r}
summary(data[food_cols])
```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(data[food_cols]))
```

```{r}
healthy_items <- c(
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fruits?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume dark green leafy vegetables?(e.g., spinach, kale, lettuce …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume whole grain products? (e.g., whole grain bread, whole grain cereal, brown rice or whole wheat pasta",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume dairy products?(e.g., milk, cheese, yogurt …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume beans?(e.g. black beans, black-eyed peas, lentils, lima beans, soy nuts …etc.)",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fish and/or shellfish (including sardines and tuna)?"
)

unhealthy_items <- c(
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume fast foods and/or pre-made or packaged food?",
  "On an average week, how many times do you consume coffee or other sources of caffeine?(e.g., soda, tea, chocolate, energy drink …etc.)"
)
```

```{r}  
data$Healthy_Score <- rowSums(data[, healthy_items], na.rm = TRUE)
data$Unhealthy_Score <- rowSums(data[, unhealthy_items], na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(data$Healthy_Score)
```

```{r}
summary(data$Unhealthy_Score)
```

```{r}
shapiro.test(data$Healthy_Score)

shapiro.test(data$Unhealthy_Score)
```

```{r}
lm_healthy_income <- lm(Healthy_Score ~ income_factor, data = data)
summary(lm_healthy_income)

lm_unhealthy_income <- lm(Unhealthy_Score ~ income_factor, data = data)
summary(lm_unhealthy_income)
```

```{r}
# Healthy plot
p1 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = income_factor, y = Healthy_Score, group = 1)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, color = "darkgreen") +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", linetype = "dashed", se = FALSE) + # trend line
  labs(title = "Income vs. Healthy Eating", 
       subtitle = "Significant Positive Trend",
       y = "Avg. Healthy Score", x = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())    

# Unhealthy plot
p2 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = income_factor, y = Unhealthy_Score, group = 1)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, color = "orange") +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1, color = "orange") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", linetype = "dashed", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Income vs. Unhealthy Eating", 
       subtitle = "No Significant Linear Trend",
       y = "Avg. Unhealthy Score", x = "Household Income") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "Less than $10,000"        = "5k",
      "$10,000 to $19,999"       = "15k",
      "$20,000 to $29,999"       = "25k",
      "$30,000 to $39,999"       = "35k",
      "$40,000 to $49,999"       = "45k",
      "$50,000 to $59,999"       = "55k",
      "$60,000 to $69,999"       = "65k",
      "$70,000 to $79,999"       = "75k",
      "$80,000 to $89,999"       = "85k",
      "$90,000 to $99,999"       = "95k",
      "$100,000 to $149,999"     = "125k",
      "$150,000 to $249,999"     = "200k",
      "$250,000 or more"         = "250k"
    )
  )

# show over each others
p1 / p2
```
